analysis_result: 17p loss was detected in 3 of 8 MPNST_G1 tumors (37.5%), based on an arm-level median log2 intensity ratio < -0.10 relative to Benign_NF_G4 reference samples. The samples with 17p loss were M2252 (17p log2 -0.29), M2346 (-0.13), and M6712 (-0.45, deepest). Thresholds -0.15 or -0.20 yield 2/8 (25%); a permissive threshold of -0.05 yields 4/8 (50%). Note: CONUMEE (R) was unavailable; this is a Python-based approximation of the arm-level workflow.
denominator_used: N = 8 samples; definition: all tumors in MOESM4 Clinical Data sheet with Methylation_Group == 'MPNST_G1' (tumor IDs M1014, M2252, M2346, M2372, M3048, M3398, M6712, M803).
assumptions_made: CONUMEE (R/Bioconductor) is unavailable (minfi/conumee failed to install due to missing gfortran/statmod/DNAcopy); a Python approximation using methylprep to read IDATs, total probe intensity summed per CpG, log2 ratio vs. 20 Benign_NF_G4 reference samples, median-centering to genome-wide 0, and arm-level median log2 ratio was used; loss threshold = -0.10 log2 (chosen to match CONUMEE default plus the mean-3SD of 59 non-tumor reference samples on 17p, which equals -0.099); hg19 centromere coordinates used for p/q arm assignment; autosomal probes only (13,085 on 17p); Benign_NF_G4 samples are diploid at 17p.
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
   - 41467_2023_38432_MOESM4_ESM.xlsx, sheet "Clinical Data": 108 samples, columns Tumor_ID, Methylation_Group, Sentrix_ID.
   - GSE207207_idats/: 216 IDAT files (108 Grn + 108 Red pairs) + EPIC manifest GPL21145_MethylationEPIC_15073387_v-1-0.csv.gz.
   - 108/108 sample IDATs successfully parsed with Python methylprep.IdatDataset.

B. COHORT DEFINITION
   - All samples with Methylation_Group == "MPNST_G1" (8 samples) were taken as the analytic cohort.
   - Reference (control) set for log2-ratio computation = 20 Benign_NF_G4 samples (benign neurofibromas, nearest normal tissue available in the dataset).

C. VARIABLE CODING / CNV PIPELINE
   - CONUMEE (R/Bioconductor) requires minfi/conumee/IlluminaHumanMethylationEPIC* packages; these are NOT installed on this machine and could not be built (BiocManager install failed: gfortran absent, statmod/DNAcopy/limma/minfi compilation failed). So a Python approximation to the conumee arm-level workflow was used.
   - Per-probe total intensity computed for each of the 108 samples from raw IDATs:
       * Type II probes: Grn(AddressA) + Red(AddressA)
       * Type I Grn probes: Grn(AddressA) + Grn(AddressB)
       * Type I Red probes: Red(AddressA) + Red(AddressB)
   - Manifest filter: 848,166 autosomal probes (chr1-22) with valid MAPINFO; p/q arm assigned from MAPINFO vs hg19 centromere coordinates. 17p = 13,085 probes, 17q = 31,450 probes.
   - Log2 per-probe intensities transformed and subtracted from per-probe mean of the 20 Benign_NF_G4 reference samples.
   - Each sample was median-centered across stable probes (ref SD <= 90th percentile) to enforce genome-wide median log2 ratio = 0, matching CONUMEE's normalization logic.
   - Arm-level log2 ratio = median of all probe log2 ratios on that arm.

D. COMPUTATION
   - Loss threshold: arm-level median log2 ratio < -0.10, i.e., >=~15 percent decrement in intensity relative to diploid reference. This is in line with CONUMEE's default "loss" call (-0.1 to -0.15 window) and equals the mean - 3 SD of the 17p arm in 59 non-tumor reference samples (mean +0.004, SD 0.034, hence threshold ~-0.10).
   - 17p arm-level log2 ratio (median of 13,085 probes) per MPNST_G1 sample:
       Tumor    17p       17q       17p-17q
       M1014    +0.064    +0.163    -0.099
       M2252    -0.289    +0.071    -0.360
       M2346    -0.130    +0.093    -0.223
       M2372    +0.020    +0.047    -0.027
       M3048    +0.053    +0.260    -0.207
       M3398    -0.037    +0.011    -0.048
       M6712    -0.447    +0.054    -0.501
       M803     -0.060    +0.148    -0.208
   - Sliding 5-Mb windows on 17p confirmed consistent arm-long loss for M2252, M2346, M6712 (all five windows < -0.1 or deep negative) and no arm-level loss for the other five MPNST_G1 tumors.

E. RESULT
   - 17p loss (arm-level median log2 ratio < -0.10) was called in 3 of 8 MPNST_G1 tumors: M2252, M2346, M6712.
   - Frequency of 17p loss in MPNST_G1: 3/8 = 37.5%.
   - Sensitivity to threshold:
       * log2 < -0.05: 4/8 (50.0%) -- adds M803, a borderline shallow loss
       * log2 < -0.10: 3/8 (37.5%)  <-- primary
       * log2 < -0.15: 2/8 (25.0%) -- drops M2346 whose arm-median just clears
       * log2 < -0.20: 2/8 (25.0%)
       * 3x SD adaptive (thr ~ -0.10): 3/8 (37.5%)
   - Relative to 17q: MPNST_G1 median 17p = -0.049 vs MPNST_G2 median 17p = +0.027 (Mann-Whitney U=18, one-sided p=0.080 -- suggestive but not significant at n=8 per group).
   - 17p loss appears enriched and often deep (M6712 has 17p log2 = -0.45, consistent with near-complete arm loss with moderate purity) in MPNST_G1 compared with benign/premalignant groups (Benign_NF_G4 median 17p = +0.002, Cutaneous_NF +0.02).

F. CAVEATS
   - BACKEND SUBSTITUTION: the official CONUMEE R workflow was unavailable. The Python pipeline approximates arm-level log2 ratios but does not implement CONUMEE's (a) quantile correction between Type I/II probes, (b) linear regression correction against reference intensity distribution, (c) circular binary segmentation (CBS) for breakpoint detection. A true CONUMEE run might call additional borderline samples (e.g., M803, arm median -0.06; 36% of 17p probes below -0.15) as loss if a CBS segment spans most of 17p. Thus 3/8 is the conservative central estimate and up to 4/8 (50%) is plausible under less stringent thresholds.
   - Reference choice: Benign_NF_G4 used as the diploid reference (20 samples). If any Benign_NF_G4 samples carry 17p CNVs themselves, that would bias ratios toward null. Inspection showed Benign_NF_G4 17p has SD of only 0.034 log2, indicating a clean reference.
   - Tumor purity: low-purity MPNST samples will show attenuated log2 ratios, so arm loss may be underestimated at any fixed threshold.
   - Centromere coordinates used (UCSC hg19 cytoBand). MPNST (GSE207207) methylation is EPIC 850k, GRCh37/hg19-annotated in manifest v-1-0.
   - Small n (8 MPNST_G1 samples) means the 37.5% point estimate has wide uncertainty (exact binomial 95% CI: 8.5-75.5%).
